Title: AI Consultation
Prompt/Description: Prompt to help you understand how AI can help you in your specific role.

Title: Analyst Customer Sentiment
Prompt/Description: Prompt that uses Analyst in Copilot or Excel to calculate sentiment score for customer feedback and analyze in charts.
Category/Tags: General; Analyst; Customer Service; Sales; Operations

Title: Analyst Generate Visual
Prompt/Description: Prompt that uses Analyst in Copilot or Excel generate unique visuals from the provided data.

Title: Analyst Sales Forecast
Prompt/Description: Prompt that uses Analyst in Copilot or Excel to forecast store sales for the next three months.

Title: Analyst Sales Trends
Prompt/Description: Prompt that uses Analyst in Copilot or Excel to identify sales trends in the provided data (or use your own data).

Title: Analyst YoY Performance
Prompt/Description: Prompt that uses Analyst in Copilot or Excel to identify top and bottom performing stores based on year-over-year growth.

Title: Board and Leadership 1:1 Prep
Prompt/Description: Builds a structured briefing for a 1:1 with a board member, chair, or direct report.
Category/Tags: General; Meetings; Summarization; Copilot and Me; Insights

Title: Competitor Campaign Radar
Prompt/Description: Scans competitor campaigns, pricing, and bundle changes in the last 30 days with recommended counter-moves.

Title: AI ROI Analysis
Prompt/Description: Prompt that uses Cowork to analyze AI initiatives for ROI and impact, and generates executive-ready Word and PowerPoint deliverables.

Title: At-Risk Client Detector
Prompt/Description: Prompt that reviews the past month of client communications for negative sentiment or relationship friction, then drafts re-engagement emails for each flagged account.
Category/Tags: Professional Services; General; Email; Advisory; Cowork

Title: Client Meeting Briefing
Prompt/Description: Prompt that generates a briefing for an upcoming client meeting and an email to the client engagement team for your review.
Category/Tags: Professional Services; Meetings; Cowork; Email; Documents

Title: Client Relationship Health
Prompt/Description: Prompt that analyzes recent emails and documents to assess client account health across portfolio. Produces a leadership-ready status report.
Category/Tags: Professional Services; Documents; Advisory; Cowork; Insights

Title: Executive Assistant
Prompt/Description: Prompt that uses Cowork to function as an Executive Assistant to identify schedule conflicts, meeting prep, and email drafting.

Title: Monthly Business Review
Prompt/Description: Prompt to generate an MBR slide with insights including trends, actions, and asks.

Title: Pending Response Tracker
Prompt/Description: Prompt that scans recent emails to surface follow-ups owed to partners and senior leaders, then drafts emails for review.
Category/Tags: General; Professional Services; Tasks; Cowork; Email

Title: Earnings Call Prep
Prompt/Description: Builds an earnings-call briefing comparing performance to core competitors and drafts likely analyst Q&A.

Title: Email Confirmation
Prompt/Description: Drafts an email to confirm understanding of request.

Title: Email Re-write
Prompt/Description: Re-write an email more concise and in a more professional tone.

Title: Emails by Topic
Prompt/Description: Helps identify important information by topic on emails sent last week.

Title: Employee Feedback
Prompt/Description: Searches all artifacts to find employee feedback sent on a direct report.

Title: Employee Performance
Prompt/Description: Helps analyize notes to produce a performance summary for an employee.

Title: Executive Regulatory Change Briefing
Prompt/Description: Summarizes recent regulatory and compliance changes across operating markets with readiness status for the risk or audit committee.

Title: Executive Thank You Note
Prompt/Description: Drafts a short, personal thank-you message from the executive for a person or team tied to a specific outcome.
Category/Tags: General; Email; People Management; Copilot and Me

Title: Executive Weekly Digest
Prompt/Description: Summarizes the executive's past week across email, chats, and meetings grouped by stakeholder theme (board, investors, regulators, customers, people).
Category/Tags: General; Copilot and Me; Insights; Summarization

Title: Executive Weekly Update Draft
Prompt/Description: Drafts a concise weekly update for the board chair, investors, or direct reports, grouped by project or topic.

Title: Project on Track
Prompt/Description: Helps determine if a project is on track based on email, Teams, and calendar invites.

Title: Followup Questions
Prompt/Description: Creates list of questions based on open items in chats/email.

Title: Generate Agent Ideas
Prompt/Description: Uses Copilot to help brainstorm agentic ideas for a specific topic, vertical, or industry that work with Agent Builder

Title: Meeting Notes
Prompt/Description: Help summarize recent meetings notes with an emphasis on decisions and open items.

Title: Meeting Prep
Prompt/Description: Helps prepare for an upcoming meeting.

Title: Meetings by Participant
Prompt/Description: Searches calendar for future meetings with specific people or companies.

Title: Plan my day
Prompt/Description: Use Copilot to help start your day, prioritizing urgent matters and based on its knowledge of your preferences and schedule.

Title: Follow-up Questions
Prompt/Description: Helps create follow-up questions based on recent chat, email, and meetings.

Title: Find Artifacts by Topic
Prompt/Description: Helps find artifacts relevant to a specific initiative.

Title: Prompt Clarifying Questions First
Prompt/Description: Meta-prompt pattern that asks Copilot to check for missing context and ask clarifying questions before answering.
Platform(s): Copilot; Outlook; Teams; Word; Excel; Researcher; Analyst

Title: Researcher Acquisition
Prompt/Description: Prompt to have Researcher perform detailed analysis on a potential aquisition.

Title: Researcher Topic Brief
Prompt/Description: Prompt to have Researcher summarize all topic-related content into an easily consumable brief.

Title: Roast my Week
Prompt/Description: Fun prompt to let Copilot roast your week based on you calendar and other knowledge in WorkIQ.

Title: Sales Customer Relationship Health
Prompt/Description: Executive health view of top customer accounts with red/amber/green ranking and next actions.
Category/Tags: General; Sales; Customer Service; Insights; Analyst

Title: Stakeholder Communication Style Coach
Prompt/Description: Analyzes a board member, investor, or peer executive's communication style from prior email and chats, and suggests how to respond.
Category/Tags: General; Email; Copilot and Me; People Management

Title: Summarize Document
Prompt/Description: Helps summarize a document into no more than 10 bullet points for quick consumption.

Title: Summarize Mentions
Prompt/Description: Help summarize emails and chats where you were mentioned yesterday.

Title: Summarize Outcomes
Prompt/Description: Summarizes open questions or decisions from chat/email.

Title: Team Recognition Nomination
Prompt/Description: Identifies employees worth nominating for recognition based on recent collaboration signals the executive has observed.
Category/Tags: General; People Management; Human Resources; Insights

Title: Teams by Topic
Prompt/Description: Searches and summarizes Teams conversations by topic.

Title: Weekly Productivity Review
Prompt/Description: Reviews last week's calendar, email, and Teams activity against what's coming up and ranks productivity suggestions for the executive.
Category/Tags: General; Copilot and Me; Insights; Tasks

Title: AskHR
Prompt/Description: AskHR is an employee-facing agent for answering policy, benefits, and role related questions for employees. It will only answer HR questions it has answers for.

Title: Client Engagement Advisor
Prompt/Description: Agent that helps professional services leaders prepare for client conversations.

Title: Competitor Research
Prompt/Description: Provides executive-ready competitor and market research using Bing Search to retrieve verified intelligence from approved competitor and retail trend websites, synthesizing findings into strategic summaries with mandatory source citations.

Title: Customer Feedback Analyzer
Prompt/Description: Agent that analyzes customer feedback and provides insights.
Category/Tags: Retail; Sales; Marketing; Operations; Customer Service

Title: Customer Health and Renewals Agent
Prompt/Description: The Customer Health and Renewals Agent helps leaders quickly understand which customers are healthy, at risk, or critical by combining usage patterns, support activity, renewal status, and relationship signals into a single, clear narrative. Instead of just showing scores, it explains why an account is in its current state, highlights renewal risk, and recommends specific next actions—producing executive-ready insights grounded in real customer data, all within a lightweight, knowledge-only agent.

Title: Employee Signals Agent
Prompt/Description: The Employee Signals Agent provides detailed analysis of 2026 employee survey responses, including ratings and comments. PII information has been removed.

Title: Legal Governance Agent
Prompt/Description: The Legal Governance Agent is an expert at our legal governance policies and procedures. Provides Q&A for employees on legal governance policies.

Title: NDA Agent
Prompt/Description: A knowledge-grounded assistant that helps Zava teams do a fast, consistent first-pass triage of incoming NDAs. It summarizes key terms, flags deviations versus Zava’s preferred positions, highlights red flags, and drafts recommended negotiation language and escalation notes. Outputs are designed for business stakeholders and always recommend review by counsel for final decisions.

Title: Perishables Agent
Prompt/Description: This agent helps grocery store management reduce perishable loss by analyzing daily perishable stock data exports. It identifies risk areas, visualizes trends, and recommends actionable mitigations such as markdowns, redistribution, and supplier adjustments. The agent uses only the provided daily export data and no external sources or assumptions.

Title: Sales Forecast Agent
Prompt/Description: Provides weekly sales forecasts for Produce, Dairy, and Frozen Foods based on historical trends and seasonal factors. The agent uses only the provided daily export data and no external sources or assumptions.

Title: Store Performance
Prompt/Description: This agent helps executives understand store performance by analyzing weekly store sales summaries. It identifies what is going well, where there are opportunities for improvements, and recommendations on actionable strategies to address. The agent uses only the provided weekly export data and no external sources or assumptions.

Title: Store and Product Analyzer
Prompt/Description: This agent helps executives analyze weekly store and product performance. It can help identify trends, opportunies, and threats. The agent uses only the provided weekly export data and no external sources or assumptions.

Title: Researcher Agent - Explore AI Solutions
Prompt/Description: Explore possible AI solutions to address [insert problem]. Focus on retrieval-based approaches using Microsoft Copilot, Copilot Studio agents, or connected knowledge sources. Summarize three possible solution approaches, their benefits, and limitations.

Title: Meeting Recap - Summarize Decisions with Owners
Prompt/Description: Resume la reunión en 5–7 puntos y convierte cada punto en una decisión con un responsable y una fecha objetivo. Si faltan datos, sugiere responsables y fechas tentativas basándote en el contexto de la conversación.

Title: Meeting Recap - Extract Explicit/Implicit Decisions
Prompt/Description: Extrae todas las decisiones explícitas e implícitas. Para cada una, sugiere responsable, fecha objetivo y primer siguiente paso.

Title: Meeting Recap - Decisions Table
Prompt/Description: Crea una tabla con: Decisión | Responsable | Fecha objetivo | Primer siguiente paso | Riesgo/Bloqueador asociado.

Title: Meeting Recap - Commitments as Tasks
Prompt/Description: ¿Quién se comprometió a qué durante la reunión? Normaliza los compromisos como tareas accionables.

Title: Meeting Recap - Risks and Mitigation Owners
Prompt/Description: Identifica riesgos y dependencias mencionadas y asigna un owner de mitigación.

Title: Meeting Recap - 30-60-90 Day Plan
Prompt/Description: Genera un plan 30–60–90 días a partir de las decisiones tomadas, con hitos y responsables.

Title: Meeting Recap - Final Decisions & Responsible Parties Table
Prompt/Description: Reestructura el resultado en una tabla. Campos: Decisión | Owner | Due date | Next step | Estado (Nuevo/En curso) | Referencia (timestamp de la transcripción).

Title: Executive Briefing (Copilot Chat)
Prompt/Description: Give me a one-page executive briefing on our top 3 strategic priorities this quarter. Reference recent emails, Teams messages, and shared files I'm involved in. Surface key risks, decisions needed from me, and recommended talking points.

Title: Leadership Memo Draft (Copilot in Word)
Prompt/Description: Draft a 1-page memo to the leadership team on our Q3 priorities. Include: a 3-sentence executive summary, top 3 priorities with owners, key risks, and a clear call-to-action for the next leadership meeting. Tone: confident, direct, and concise.

Title: Leadership Deck Build (Copilot in PowerPoint)
Prompt/Description: Create a presentation from [Link to leadership memo]. Keep slides minimal — 4 bullets max per slide. Include a title slide, executive summary, priorities, risks, and an 'asks of leadership' closing slide.

Title: Summarize Earnings Call Transcript
Prompt/Description: Summarize the key points from the Microsoft FY24 Second Quarter Earnings Conference Call.

Title: Summarize Specific Speaker Remarks
Prompt/Description: Provide a brief summary of Satya Nadella's remarks during the earnings call.

Title: Comprehensive Earnings Call Analysis
Prompt/Description: Analyze the Microsoft FY24 Second Quarter Earnings Conference Call document to provide a comprehensive report that includes: a summary of key points from each speaker; identification of the top three growth areas and their contributing factors; a detailed breakdown of financial performance, including revenue, operating income, and earnings per share; trends in AI adoption and its influence on Microsoft's business strategy; a comparison of this quarter's performance with the same quarter last year, highlighting significant changes; key strategic initiatives and future outlook as discussed in the call.

Title: Executive Summary from Pasted Content
Prompt/Description: Based on the following information, provide an executive summary on the following information: [paste the Word output here]

Title: Condense Executive Summary
Prompt/Description: Summarize this executive summary into a more concise format by focusing on the most critical insights and metrics for each speaker. Use a structured format with headings and bullet points to improve readability. Export to a Word document.

Title: Generate Talking Points for Manager
Prompt/Description: Based on the summarized executive summary, generate 5-7 concise and impactful talking points my manager can use in their next leadership call. Focus on key achievements, growth areas, and strategic priorities.

Title: Schedule Follow-Up Meeting
Prompt/Description: I need to schedule a 30-minute meeting with [/Pick a colleague] tomorrow afternoon to discuss the Second Quarter Earnings Conference Call. Can you suggest a time that works? If they are unavailable, provide an alternative.

Title: Draft Meeting Agenda
Prompt/Description: I'm meeting with my boss to discuss key updates and strategic initiatives they missed from the Second Quarter Earnings Conference Call. Create an agenda to discuss financial performance, AI and technology integration, strategic acquisitions, productivity updates, and future outlook.

Title: Two-Minute Voice Briefing
Prompt/Description: Give me a two-minute verbal briefing on what I missed since Friday — what decision is most urgent, what's blocked on me, and what one positive signal I should walk into Monday knowing.

Title: Earnings-Call Brief (Researcher Agent)
Prompt/Description: Summarize the most recent earnings call for [Energizer Holdings / Church & Dwight / Kenvue / Procter & Gamble — pick one]. Highlight: key financial results and drivers; the major strategic priorities leadership chose to emphasize; the analyst questions and concerns that came up, especially ones management dodged; the risks, headwinds, and market pressures named; the implications for Edgewell — where it threatens our position, where it reveals an opening, and the one question this raises for our own strategy.

Title: Multi-Agent Strategic Teardown
Prompt/Description: Researcher: pull the most credible public view on the U.S. mineral SPF face category — leaders, growth, retailer signals. Analyst: pull Edgewell sun-care performance from our 10-K and compare to the relevant peer segments at Energizer, Church & Dwight, and Kenvue. Bring both into one Notebook. Give me a two-page teardown: where we're winning, where we're losing, the single strategic move I'd argue for to the board, and the risk that move carries. Cite every number.

Title: Meeting Decisions & Owners Block
Prompt/Description: From this meeting, generate the decisions-and-owners block I can post to the chat right now: decisions made — specific commitments, not themes; action items — named owner, specific next step, due date; open questions we're carrying forward; the one decision we explicitly chose not to make today and when we'll revisit it.

Title: Cowork: Draft & Schedule Follow-Up Emails
Prompt/Description: Draft the follow-up to each meeting owner with their specific commitment, schedule it to send at 8 AM tomorrow, and flag any that touch a customer.

Title: Cowork: Assemble Board Deck
Prompt/Description: Assemble the board deck from the Banana Boat teardown and this morning's brand review. Use Edgewell brand standards. Stage it in my SharePoint.

Title: Cowork: Schedule Leadership Kickoff
Prompt/Description: Get the 90-day plan kickoff on calendars this week — Brand President, FP&A lead, Head of Sales, CMO. 45 minutes. Use the brief as the agenda.

Title: Pages 101: Draft Working Brief
Prompt/Description: Based on what you know about my role at Edgewell, my brands, and what's happening in the personal care category, draft me a working brief titled "Banana Boat — what we change in the next 90 days." Structure it as: one-sentence point of view; three things we know are true; three things we think are true but should validate; the move I'd argue for; the open questions I need brand, sales, insights, and FP&A to answer before we commit. Keep it editable — short sentences, no flourish.

Title: Pages 101: Co-Edit Synthesis
Prompt/Description: Look at the comments and edits my teammates have made in the last 15 minutes. Summarize where we agree, where we disagree, and the single most substantive challenge that was raised. Then propose a revised paragraph 4 (the move) that incorporates the strongest pushback. Mark anything you changed so we can see the diff.

Title: Multi-Agent Working Session: Orchestrate
Prompt/Description: For the next 10 minutes, I want to work this Page with my teammate and with two agents helping. Researcher: pull the most current public view on the U.S. mineral SPF face category — growth, leaders (Supergoop, EltaMD, Sun Bum mineral face, Neutrogena Sheer Zinc), retailer signals. Cite. Analyst: separately, pull Edgewell's Banana Boat face-SPF revenue, units, and ad spend from any internal files in our tenant — also pull what's publicly disclosed for Edgewell sun-care in our most recent 10-K. Coordinate so you're not duplicating work. Put your findings into the Page under a new section called "Evidence."
Platform(s): Copilot Pages; Analyst Agent; Researcher Agent

Title: Multi-Agent Working Session: Direct the Room
Prompt/Description: My teammate has just commented that the Walmart relationship is the binding constraint, not the consumer demand. Researcher — find any public evidence about Walmart's SPF planogram changes, mineral SPF buyer rotation, or pricing dynamics in mass sun care. Analyst — find any internal Edgewell Walmart JBP files or 4-blocker notes that touch sun care. Both: put findings in a new Page section called "Walmart channel reality." Then I'll synthesize.
Platform(s): Copilot Pages; Analyst Agent; Researcher Agent

Title: Multi-Agent Working Session: Close & Summarize
Prompt/Description: Together with my teammate, summarize this Page as it stands now in 6 bullets: what we now believe; what changed in our thinking in the last 15 minutes; the move we're going to argue for; the strongest single piece of evidence supporting it; the strongest pushback we expect from FP&A and our response; the three open questions we still need to answer before we commit. Highlight which agent contributed which evidence so we can revisit sources.

Title: Brand Agent: Instructions Setup
Prompt/Description: You are the Schick Brand Agent. You answer questions only from the files in this SharePoint site, which contains Schick's growth plans, JBP decks, scan-data reports, agency briefs, S&OP recaps, and competitive trackers from the last 24 months. Behavior rules: Always cite the specific file and page/slide. If a question can be answered from older content (>12 months), flag that it may be stale. When asked about competitors (Gillette, Harry's, BIC), only use internal files plus quoted public sources — do not invent. When asked about financials, ground in the brand P&L and 4-blockers, never estimate. If a question can't be answered from site content, say so and suggest who likely has the answer. Use Edgewell terminology; treat "shave system" and "disposable" as distinct categories. Audience: Schick brand team, sales partners, insights team, executives.

Title: Brand Agent: Starter Prompts Setup
Prompt/Description: Set the following five starter prompts on this agent so they appear as suggested questions when anyone opens it: 1. What's Schick's current point of view on private-label competition in disposables? 2. Summarize our most recent JBP commitments with Walmart for Schick. 3. What did our last consumer research say about Gen-Z and wet shave? 4. Show me Schick's YTD performance vs. plan, and the biggest gap. 5. What changed in the last 60 days that I should know about?

Title: Brand Agent: New-Hire Test Questions
Prompt/Description: Pretend I'm a new brand manager who started Monday on the Schick team. I have three questions: what is the brand's current strategic position in one paragraph; who are the three most important retailers and what is the open issue with each; what consumer insight from the last year has most shaped how we're going to market this year. Cite everything.

Title: Copilot Studio: Agent Product Brief
Prompt/Description: Help me write a product brief for a custom Copilot Studio agent at Edgewell. The process I want to agentify is: [name the process — e.g., Walmart JBP Prep]. Cover: WHO uses this agent and what role they're in; WHAT problem the agent solves in one sentence, with the alternative-today (current pain) named; WHAT inputs the agent asks for (specific fields); WHAT data sources it draws from (name actual SharePoint sites, files, systems); WHAT artifact it produces; FAILURE MODES — three ways this agent could be wrong, and how we'd detect; SUCCESS METRIC — what would make us say in 90 days "this was worth it." Keep it to one page. Executive product-owner tone.

Title: Copilot Studio: JBP Prep Agent Instructions
Prompt/Description: You are the JBP Prep Agent for Edgewell sales leads. Your job is to walk a sales lead through preparing for a Joint Business Plan meeting with a retailer (Walmart, Target, Costco, Amazon, CVS, Walgreens, Kroger, Club, or international). When a user starts, ask: which retailer is the JBP for; which brands are in scope; the JBP date; what they remember being committed to in the last JBP. Then: pull the most recent JBP deck, last year's commitments, current YTD performance, open chargebacks, and category competitive context; produce a structured prep document with sections — "Where we stand," "What we owe them," "What we want from them," "Tension points," "Open data we still need"; ask three sharpening questions before finalizing; output the final prep doc to a new Page in the user's OneDrive titled "[Retailer] JBP Prep — [Date]." Tone: efficient, retailer-savvy, never breezy. Never estimate if a number's source can't be found.
Category/Tags: Executives; Agent Builder; Copilot Studio; Sales

Title: Copilot Studio: Pressure-Test Agent Design
Prompt/Description: Critique this Copilot Studio agent design from the perspective of a senior Edgewell sales lead who'd actually use it. Where is the conversation flow weak? Where is the prep doc structure likely to feel useless? What inputs am I asking for that don't matter? What inputs am I NOT asking for that I should? What's the one change that would make the difference between this getting used and this getting ignored after week 2?

Title: Capstone: Generate 90-Day Plan Spine
Prompt/Description: The board endorsed our recommendation: [paste the one-sentence recommendation from your Part I D3 brief]. Generate the spine of a 90-day execution plan to deliver against it. Structure: Section 1 — the single goal we're driving to (one sentence, with the success metric). Section 2 — the three workstreams that get us there, each with desired end-state at day 90, named owner, first three milestones with dates, and the one risk that would derail it. Section 3 — weekly operating rhythm: who meets, when, what they're accountable for. Section 4 — the leading indicators we'll watch (not lagging — leading). Section 5 — decisions we're explicitly NOT making now, and when we'll revisit. Output as the structure of the Page itself, with placeholders my team will fill in.

Title: Capstone: Orchestrate Multi-Role Working Session
Prompt/Description: For the next 12 minutes, here is who owns what in this Page. ANALYST AGENT: take the success metric and leading indicators, pull baseline numbers from our brand P&L, propose three leading indicators with current values and 90-day targets, cite sources. BRAND AGENT: take the "where we stand" context — pull the most recent JBP commitments, current promo calendar, open chargebacks/recalls, and three biggest unresolved tensions with retail partners. RESEARCHER AGENT: take the "external watchlist" — three competitor moves to track in the next 90 days, with reasoning. BRAND PRESIDENT: fill in Workstream 1 (brand-side moves). FP&A LEAD: fill in Workstream 2 (financial commitments and gates). HEAD OF SALES: fill in Workstream 3 (retailer execution). CMO: write the one-sentence goal and pressure-test our success metric. Everyone work simultaneously. I'll synthesize at minute 12.
Platform(s): Copilot Pages; Analyst Agent; Researcher Agent; SharePoint Agent

Title: Capstone: Synthesize Plan Conflicts
Prompt/Description: Synthesize across everything humans and agents have put into this Page in the last 12 minutes. Tell me: where the workstreams are mutually reinforcing — call out specific dependencies; where the workstreams contradict each other or compete for the same resource — name the conflict; which milestones have the same owner — flag any concentration risk; which leading indicators are most defensible vs. aspirational; the single thing this plan is implicitly assuming that, if untrue, breaks the whole thing. Be direct. The CFO will read this on Friday.

Title: Capstone: Self-Maintaining Plan Sections
Prompt/Description: Set up two sections of this Page to refresh automatically every Monday morning. "This week's read on the leading indicators" — the Analyst Agent should pull the current value of each indicator, compare to plan, and flag anything off track in red with a 2-sentence interpretation. "External watchlist update" — the Researcher Agent should check for any new credible signal on the three competitor moves being tracked and post a 3-bullet update. Both should be timestamped and cite sources. Notify @[me] and @[brand president] when they refresh.
Platform(s): Copilot Pages; Analyst Agent; Researcher Agent

Title: Capstone: Generate Commitment Block
Prompt/Description: In the final 2 minutes, produce the commitment block for the bottom of this Page. List, by named person: what they own at day 30; what they own at day 60; what they own at day 90. Make sure each entry is a specific verb (deliver, decide, commit, ship) not a vague verb (review, discuss, look into). Then add: "We agreed not to revisit this plan until day 30 unless a leading indicator goes red."

Title: Voice Catch-Up Briefing
Prompt/Description: I just spent three days at our Springdale poultry plant and a beef facility, plus a buyer line review in Bentonville. Give me a verbal briefing in two minutes: what's the most urgent thing waiting for me, what decisions are blocked on me, and what one piece of good news I should know before the 7 AM ops sync. Keep it conversational, not a list.

Title: Voice Catch-Up Follow-Up
Prompt/Description: Pause on that last point. Who exactly is asking for the decision, what's the dollar exposure or volume impact, and is a customer commitment or a plant restart waiting on me?

Title: Voice Catch-Up Closing (Ops Sync Prep)
Prompt/Description: Based on everything that came in while I was in plants and at the customer, what are the three themes I should walk into the Monday ops sync prepared to discuss across chicken, beef/pork, and prepared foods? Give me a one-sentence point of view for each.

Title: Outlook Mobile Thread Triage
Prompt/Description: Summarize this thread in five bullets. Then tell me: what is the actual ask of me, what's the deadline, what's the volume or margin at stake, and is there a position the team has already converged on that I can either endorse or push back on?

Title: Teams Catch-Up After Travel
Prompt/Description: I've been off Teams for three days while in plants. Summarize my unread messages across all my chats and channels. Group them into: needs my decision (with dollar/volume impact if known); FYI from my direct reports; customer or supplier escalations; recognition or noise I can ignore.

Title: Role Catch-Up: CEO / Senior Leader
Prompt/Description: Summarize board, investor, and external-affairs communications from the past week. Highlight anything that needs a senior leader response before the next earnings prep or board call. Include any media mentions of Tyson Foods, our brands, or our peers (Pilgrim's, Cargill, JBS, Hormel).

Title: Role Catch-Up: CFO / Finance Leader
Prompt/Description: Pull together finance-related emails — highlight chicken, beef, and pork segment commentary, quarterly close updates, grain and energy hedging notes, and any tariff or trade discussions. Prioritize anything with a dollar amount, a covenant trigger, or a deadline attached.

Title: Role Catch-Up: Chief Customer / Sales Officer
Prompt/Description: Catch me up on communications from top retail and food service customers — Walmart, Kroger, Costco, McDonald's, Chick-fil-A, Sysco, US Foods. Any promotional calendar changes, listing decisions, service complaints, or buyer feedback that needs my attention this week.

Title: Role Catch-Up: Chief Marketing / Brand Officer
Prompt/Description: Summarize brand and consumer communications across Tyson, Jimmy Dean, Hillshire Farm, Ball Park, and Hillshire Reserve. Flag campaign performance, social sentiment shifts, retail merchandising issues, and any innovation gates that need my sign-off.

Title: Role Catch-Up: COO / Operations Leader
Prompt/Description: Summarize plant operations communications — production status, downtime events, yield variances, food safety holds, USDA inspection notes, and any labor or staffing escalations from the past three days across chicken, beef, pork, and prepared foods.

Title: Role Catch-Up: Supply Chain Leader
Prompt/Description: Catch me up on live supply, grain procurement, cold-chain logistics, and DC throughput. Flag any carrier escalations, refrigerated capacity issues, or supplier risk events. Prioritize anything affecting customer fill rates this week.

Title: Role Catch-Up: Chief People Officer
Prompt/Description: Flag any team member, labor, safety, or community communications — plant safety incidents, pending labor negotiations, EHS metrics, plant town hall feedback, or community impact items that arrived in the last three days.

Title: Set Personal Context (Copilot Memory)
Prompt/Description: Remember this about me for all future conversations: I'm a senior leader at Tyson Foods. My segment is [Chicken/Beef/Pork/Prepared Foods/International]. My top customers are [list]. My direct reports include [list]. I have a weekly business review, a monthly operating review, and quarterly earnings prep on set schedules. When you draft for me, default to: direct, decision-first, no marketing fluff. Lead with the recommendation, then evidence, then risks. Use plain English a plant manager and a Wall Street analyst would both understand.

Title: Set Stakeholder Map (Copilot Memory)
Prompt/Description: Also remember my stakeholder context: my CEO cares most about [top three priorities]; my CFO is focused on margin recovery in [segment]; our board is watching [strategic priority]; our top customer's category manager values [data, on-time delivery, joint business planning]. When I draft anything to these stakeholders, tailor the tone and depth to them by default.

Title: Prompt Pattern: Brief Me
Prompt/Description: Brief me on [topic] in 200 words or less. Tell me: what changed, why it matters to Tyson, what the team is recommending, and what decision I'll need to make. Cite the documents or emails you pulled from.

Title: Prompt Pattern: Steelman Both Sides
Prompt/Description: My team is recommending [decision]. Steelman the case for it in 100 words and the case against it in 100 words. Then tell me what data I'd need to break the tie.

Title: Prompt Pattern: So-What
Prompt/Description: Read this report/email/deck. Skip the summary — give me the three "so-whats" for my segment and one question I should be asking that nobody in the document is asking.

Title: Prompt Pattern: Draft in My Voice
Prompt/Description: Draft this in my voice: direct, no fluff, lead with the point, use plant-floor plain English. Three short paragraphs max. End with a clear ask or next step.

Title: Prompt Pattern: Prep Me for the Room
Prompt/Description: I have a meeting with [person/team] at [time]. Pull the last 60 days of email and Teams I have with them. Tell me: their top concern right now, what I committed to last time, what's outstanding, and the three questions they're most likely to ask me.

Title: Analyst: Build the Margin Story
Prompt/Description: You are an Analyst. Pull from the Prepared Foods segment files in my OneDrive and the last four quarterly close decks. Build me a margin bridge from Q3 last year to this quarter. Break it into: volume, mix (Jimmy Dean vs Hillshire Farm vs Ball Park vs Hillshire Reserve), price/promo, raw material (pork belly, pork trim, turkey), freight, labor, and plant productivity. Show me the three largest movers in dollars. Cite the source files for every line.

Title: Analyst: Margin Story Refinement
Prompt/Description: Now write three sentences of CFO-grade narrative for the largest unfavorable mover, including what the team is doing about it in the next two quarters. Flag any assumptions you made and the data points I should personally double-check before this goes into the earnings prep deck.

Title: Researcher: Read the Market
Prompt/Description: You are a Researcher. Pull the last 90 days of public commentary, earnings transcripts, and analyst notes on Hormel and Conagra Brands. I want: what they say their growth bets are in refrigerated and frozen prepared foods, where they say they're losing, and any pricing action they've announced. Then cross-reference with Tyson's last earnings call — where are they pointing and where are we pointing? Give me the three places we'd be wise to look harder.

Title: Researcher: Commodity Read
Prompt/Description: Researcher: pull the latest USDA WASDE report, current CME live cattle, lean hog, and corn futures, and any major news on grain stocks or avian influenza in the last 30 days. Summarize the implications for Tyson's chicken, beef, and pork segments over the next two quarters in 200 words. End with "the question this raises for our hedging strategy is…"

Title: Excel: Build Customer Profitability Cube
Prompt/Description: In this workbook of top-10 customer sales: build a view that shows each customer's volume, net price per lb, trade spend %, gross margin %, and YoY change for the last 8 quarters. Flag every customer where trade spend grew faster than volume. Then explain in plain English which two accounts are the biggest profitability risk and why.

Title: Excel: Build CFO Headline Chart
Prompt/Description: Now build a single chart that tells the story I'd put in front of my CFO in 30 seconds. Title it like a headline, not a label. Save the file and tell me exactly what changed so I can audit it.

Title: Teams Recap: Customer JBP Meeting
Prompt/Description: Recap the customer JBP meeting we just had. Give me: the buyer's top three concerns in their words; every commitment I made and to whom, with the date I said I'd deliver; any commitment the buyer made to us; the two unresolved items that need a follow-up call. Tone: factual, not promotional.

Title: Draft Customer Follow-Up Email
Prompt/Description: Draft a follow-up email to the buyer in my voice. Restate what we heard them say (so they know we listened), confirm the four commitments I made with dates, ask explicitly for the two things they committed to us, and propose a 30-minute mid-month check-in. Keep it under 200 words.

Title: Internal Cascade to Account Team
Prompt/Description: Now draft a Teams post for my account team channel: summarize the meeting in five bullets, assign each of my four commitments to the right function (supply chain, brand, innovation, finance), tag the owner, and ask them to confirm the date by end of day.

Title: Plant Town Hall Prep
Prompt/Description: I'm doing a town hall at the [plant location] facility next week. Pull the last 90 days of plant performance data, safety incidents, and the most recent team member engagement survey results for that site. Draft me a 10-minute talk that: opens with thanks to specific shift teams, names the two performance wins, addresses the two honest concerns the survey raised, and ends with what we're going to do differently. Plain English. Plant-floor tone, not corporate.

Title: Executive Briefing: CEO Read-Out
Prompt/Description: Draft a half-page brief for the CEO on this week in my segment. Three sections: Customer — what the top accounts told us and what we owe them; Operations — fill rate, yield, and safety vs. plan; Decisions I need from you. Use bullets, no adjectives. Cite where the data came from.

Title: Agent Charter: Customer Health Agent
Prompt/Description: Draft a one-page charter for the "Customer Health Agent." Cover: the business problem it solves; the data it needs and the systems it must connect to; what it does autonomously vs. what it escalates to a human; the metrics that prove it's working in 90 days; the risks and human-in-the-loop controls; who owns it. Use language a non-technical executive sponsor can approve.

Title: Agent Prototype: Commodity Pulse Agent
Prompt/Description: For the Commodity Pulse Agent: draft the exact daily prompt the agent should run, the sources it should pull from, the format of the morning brief (email vs. Teams post vs. mobile push), and three example outputs for chicken, beef, and pork segment leaders. Keep each output to 150 words.

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Voice Catch-Up Briefing
Prompt/Description: I just landed after one week of travel visiting facilities and meeting with key customers. Give me a verbal briefing in two minutes: what's the most urgent thing waiting for me, what decisions are blocked on me, and what one piece of good news I should know before Monday. Keep it conversational, not a list.

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Voice Catch-Up Follow-Up
Prompt/Description: Pause on that last point. Who exactly is asking for the decision, what's the deadline, and is anything on hold because I haven't responded?

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Voice Catch-Up Closing
Prompt/Description: Based on everything that came in while I was traveling, what are the three themes I should walk into Monday's executive staff meeting prepared to discuss? Give me a one-sentence point of view for each.

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Outlook Mobile Thread Triage
Prompt/Description: Summarize this thread in five bullets. Then tell me: what is the actual ask of me, what's the deadline, and is there a position the team has already converged on that I can either endorse or push back on?

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Teams Catch-Up After Travel
Prompt/Description: I've been off Teams for one week. Summarize my unread messages across all my chats. Group them into: needs my decision, FYI from my direct reports, congratulations or recognition I should respond to, noise I can ignore.

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Role Catch-Up: Executive / Senior Leader
Prompt/Description: Summarize board-related communications or investor inquiries from the past week. Highlight anything that needs a senior leader response before Monday's staff meeting. Include any media mentions of [your company] or key brands.

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Role Catch-Up: Finance Leader
Prompt/Description: Pull together finance-related emails — highlight budget approvals pending, quarterly close updates, or tariff discussions. Prioritize anything with a dollar amount or deadline attached.

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Role Catch-Up: Brand / Marketing Leader
Prompt/Description: Catch me up on communications from retail partners and channel teams. Any promotional calendar changes, listing decisions, or buyer feedback that needs my attention this week.

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Role Catch-Up: IT / Technology Leader
Prompt/Description: Summarize IT-related communications — system incidents, security alerts, project milestone updates, and vendor escalations from the past week. Prioritize anything affecting production systems or user-facing services.

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Role Catch-Up: Human Resources Leader
Prompt/Description: Flag any HR, legal, or regulatory communications — pending contracts, compliance issues, employee relations matters, or organizational changes that arrived in the last week.

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Role Catch-Up: Operations / Supply Chain Leader
Prompt/Description: Summarize communications about manufacturing operations and any supplier escalations. Include production status updates, logistics delays, and quality incidents from the past week.

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Custom Instructions: All Roles Base Template
Prompt/Description: I am a senior executive at [your company]. Our portfolio includes multiple product lines across several categories. Our top channels include major retailers and e-commerce. Always: draft email replies in a warm but executive tone — direct, no filler, no exclamation points; when summarizing, lead with the decision needed, not the chronology; if you reference data, name the source thread or document; prefer brevity — three sentences over five — unless I ask for depth; if a reply would commit [your company] to a number, a date, or a public statement, flag it for me before sending.

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Custom Instructions: Executive / Senior Leader
Prompt/Description: I am a senior executive at [your company]. I manage a portfolio of brands across multiple categories. My priorities are organic growth, margin expansion, and portfolio simplification. When drafting communications, use a strategic but approachable tone. Always flag anything that could be material to investors. I prefer one-page summaries with a clear recommendation at the top.

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Custom Instructions: Finance Leader
Prompt/Description: I am the finance leader at [your company]. My focus areas are EBITDA margin improvement, working capital optimization, tariff impact analysis, and capital allocation across our brand portfolio. When summarizing, always include the financial impact. Use precise numbers, not ranges. Flag any commitment that creates a liability or changes our guidance. Format financial data in tables when possible.

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Custom Instructions: Brand / Marketing Leader
Prompt/Description: I lead brand and marketing at [your company]. I own the P&L for key markets. My key retail partners include major national retailers and e-commerce platforms. When summarizing, focus on sell-through data, promotional ROI, and competitive shelf dynamics. I care about category share, not just brand share. Be direct — I want recommendations, not options.

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Custom Instructions: IT / Technology Leader
Prompt/Description: I lead IT and technology at [your company]. My priorities are digital transformation, cybersecurity posture, ERP modernization, and enabling AI adoption across the organization. When summarizing, include system impact, timeline, and risk level. Flag security incidents and compliance gaps immediately. Format project status in tables with RAG indicators.

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Custom Instructions: Human Resources Leader
Prompt/Description: I lead HR and people operations at [your company]. I oversee talent acquisition, employee relations, compliance, and organizational development. When drafting, be precise on legal language — do not paraphrase contractual terms. Flag regulatory risks, employment law issues, and anything requiring board approval. Keep a professional, measured tone. Always note jurisdiction when legal issues are geography-specific.

Page/Section: Edgewell ELT Copilot Experience (From Field to Forecast)
Title: Custom Instructions: Operations / Supply Chain Leader
Prompt/Description: I lead global supply chain at [your company]. Our manufacturing spans multiple facilities across several regions. Key concerns: manufacturing efficiency, supplier diversification, logistics costs, and inventory optimization. When summarizing, include facility impact, SKU-level detail, and timeline. Always flag single-source supplier risks.
Category/Tags: Executives; Operations; Supply Chain; Custom Instructions

Make each prompt specific enough that I can paste it and get a useful answer immediately.

For group 1, draft a short reply for each.

Title: Action Items Across Channels

Prompt/Description: I was traveling for the past week. Find updates and related communications from my team about [initiative or project name]. Include any decisions made while I was out and any open items waiting for my input.

Title: Document Finder & Version Check
Prompt/Description: Find the most recent version of our [document name, e.g. strategic plan or annual operating plan]. Who last edited it, when, and what were the key changes from the prior version?

Title: Cross-Reference Consistency Check

For each trend, include: evidence of consumer adoption; which of our brands or product lines is best positioned to respond; competitive moves already made by key players; the strategic implication for our portfolio. Provide sourced findings.

Title: Notebook: Competitive Teardown
Prompt/Description: Compare [your company]'s latest annual report against key competitors. For each competitor, analyze: revenue scale, growth rate, margin profile, R&D investment, and stated strategic priorities. Where are we winning? Where are we most vulnerable? What's the one move a board member would challenge us to make?

Title: Excel Agent: Business Dashboard
Prompt/Description: Using the attached business performance data, build a dashboard showing: revenue trends by product line and region; top-performing and underperforming segments; year-over-year growth rates; key insights the leadership team should discuss at the next quarterly review.

Highlight any cross-functional dependencies. Draft a follow-up message for the leadership team that recaps decisions and next steps, ready to send in Teams.

Title: Meeting Decisions & Owners Block
Prompt/Description: From this meeting, generate the decisions-and-owners block: decisions made — specific commitments, not themes; action items — named owner, specific next step, due date; open questions we're carrying forward; the one decision we explicitly chose not to make today and when we'll revisit it.

Title: Outlook Inbox Scan & Draft
Prompt/Description: Scan my inbox and identify emails that need my reply. For each one, draft a response that's consistent with my communication style. Group them: Focus Now (up to 5 items that need my personal response today), Delegate (I'll forward with a one-liner), and Acknowledge (a simple "thank you" or "noted" is sufficient). Stage the Focus Now drafts for my review.
